description:
balls bills itself as a tool you could in principle do with just jq, git, and ln. That claim still holds architecturally (git is shelled out, not linked; serde_json is the jq; symlinks are std), but the dependency tree has drifted to 41 crates / 3.5MB as features landed. This epic tracks pulling the edges back toward the primitives.

WHY THIS MATTERS: the minimal-footprint promise is a real part of what balls is — it is why the tool is auditable and trustworthy as core dev infrastructure. Every crate in the tree is attack surface, build time, and a thing a skeptical adopter has to vet. Three dependency chains currently earn their keep poorly; this epic cuts the two that are free and forces an explicit decision on the third.

Out of scope by decision: the plugin subsystem's raw-libc use (process-group kill, cloexec pipes in src/plugin/limits.rs + diag.rs) is a genuine step beyond jq/git/ln, but it is a critical feature group with a clear, deliberate line of demarcation — that clarity is the point, so it stays. Audit findings from session 'gleaning' 2026-05-19.
title: Dependency footprint audit: hold the jq+git+ln line
